sections.paragraph:
- Original: Ἑρμᾶς

- Transliteration: hermas

- Phonetic: her-mas'

- Definition: Hermas = "Mercury"

a. a Christian mentioned in Rom. 16:14. According to tradition, he was one of the seventy disciples, and afterwards bishop of Dalmatia. (A.D.)

- Origin: probably from G2060

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Probably from G2060; Hermas a Christian: - Hermas.
